scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at Bengaluru, was to be performed at Bengaluru, and the breach occurred at Bengaluru; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

COMMERCIAL COURTS ACT COMPLIANCE

3. The Plaintiff states that the present suit is subject to the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The Plaintiff has [complied with Section 12A pre-litigation mediation / is exempt from said requirement as the dispute pertains to residential property for personal use].

LIMITATION

4. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant regarding Flat No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-, as per Annexure A.

6. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant via RTGS (UTR: HDFC20240110987654), evidenced by Annexure B.

7. The remaining balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was agreed to be paid on or before 10.07.2024 against the execution of the sale deed.

8. The Plaintiff has arranged the funds for the balance consideration and has remained ready and willing to perform her part of the contract since the date of the agreement.

BREACH OF CONTRACT AND PARTICULARS

9. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the sale deed, stating that he had received a higher offer for the property, as per Annexure D.

10. On 20.06.2024, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant calling upon him to execute the sale deed in terms of the agreement, as per Annexure C.

11. The Defendant has failed and refused to execute the sale deed despite the legal notice and the Plaintiff's readiness to pay the balance consideration.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

12. The Plaintiff has been deprived of the use of the advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- due to the Defendant's refusal to perform, causing loss of interest on said amount.

13. The Plaintiff incurred expenses towards the legal notice dated 20.06.2024, amounting to Rs. {{LEGAL_NOTICE_COST}}/-, due to the Defendant's breach.

14. The Plaintiff has suffered further loss and damage due to the non-execution of the sale deed, the quantification of which is being assessed at Rs. {{QUANTIFIED_DAMAGE_AMOUNT}}/-.

LEGAL BASIS

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

16. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

17. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to enforce the contract by specific performance to accrue.

18. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

19. The suit is valued at Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of the suit property in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
    (b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (if not already in possession);
    (c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
    (d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Section 34 CPC;
    (e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt with UTR: HDFC20240110987654

Annexure C — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

Annexure D — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 19 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

Anita Sharma

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A Company registered under the Companies Act, 2013,
Having its registered office at: MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon, aged 40 years.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A Limited Liability Partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008,
Having its registered office at: Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged 48 years.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit is valued above the specified value as prescribed under Section 12 of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at Bengaluru, was to be performed at Bengaluru, and the breach occurred at Bengaluru; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain commercial disputes under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

4. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

5. The Plaintiff states that pre-institution mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 was conducted through the Bengaluru Mediation Centre. The mediation concluded unsuccessfully on {{MEDIATION_END_DATE}} and the mediation certificate is enclosed herewith. Alternatively, if mediation was not conducted, the Plaintiff seeks urgent interim relief which exempts this requirement under Section 12A provisos.

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd entered into a written Software Development Agreement with the Defendant CloudBridge Systems LLP for the development of an ERP system.

7. The total contract value was agreed upon at Rs. 45,00,000/- as per the terms of the Agreement marked as Annexure A.

8. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff paid the first milestone payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant via NEFT.

9. The said payment transaction bears UTR No. ICIC20240201456789, and the receipt thereof is marked as Annexure B.

10. As per the terms of the Agreement, the Defendant was obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024.

11. The Defendant failed to deliver the module by the stipulated deadline.

12. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without returning the advance payment, as evidenced by email correspondence marked as Annexure C.

13. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant, marked as Annexure E, to which no satisfactory response was received.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

14. The Plaintiff claims refund of the advance payment amounting to Rs. 20,00,000/- paid to the Defendant.

15. The Plaintiff suffered consequential damages of Rs. 12,00,000/- due to delay in operations caused by non-delivery, as assessed in the auditor report marked as Annexure D.

16. The Plaintiff took reasonable steps to mitigate the loss suffered.

17. The Plaintiff claims interest at 18% per annum on the total claimed amount of Rs. 32,00,000/-.

LEGAL BASIS

18. The Defendant has committed a repudiatory breach by refusing to perform its obligations, entitling the Plaintiff to treat the contract as at an end and seek appropriate relief.

19. The Plaintiff is entitled to compensation under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby, which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach, or which the parties knew, when they made the contract, to be likely to result from the breach of it.

CAUSE OF ACTION

20. The cause of action first arose on 01.05.2024 when the Defendant failed to deliver the module by the contractual deadline and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to sue for damages to accrue.

21. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for damages subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

22. The suit is valued at Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to the ad valorem rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. 32,00,000/- as damages for breach of contract;
    (b) Award pendente lite interest at the rate of 18% per annum from the date of breach / institution of suit till decree under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (c) Award future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till realisation under Section 34 CPC;
    (d) Award costs of the suit including advocate's fees to the Plaintiff;
    (e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Refund of advance payment
     Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
     Basis / methodology: First milestone payment under Software Development Agreement
     Documentary proof: Annexure B (NEFT receipt)

(ii) Consequential damages
     Amount claimed: Rs. 12,00,000/-
     Basis / methodology: Loss assessment by auditor due to operational delay
     Documentary proof: Annexure D (Auditor report)

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. 32,00,000/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure B — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

Annexure C — Email correspondence showing abandonment

Annexure D — Loss assessment report by auditor

Annexure E — Legal notice dated 01.07.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at Bengaluru on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, Priya Menon, the Director and authorised representative of the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 22 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

Priya Menon

DIRECTOR

TECHSOFT SOLUTIONS PVT LTD

STATEMENT OF TRUTH

I, Priya Menon, the Director and authorised representative of the Plaintiff herein, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that the contents of the above plaint and the facts stated in paragraphs 1 to 22 are true to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

I understand that I shall be liable to proceedings under law for making a false statement.

Verified at Bengaluru on th
